sn43.12:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, I will teach you the unconditioned and the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.12:1.4.eng: The ending of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn43.12:1.6.eng: And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.12:1.8.eng: This is called the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.12:1.9.pli: Iti kho, bhikkhave, desitaṁ vo mayā asaṅkhataṁ, desito asaṅkhatagāmimaggo.
sn43.12:1.9.eng: So, mendicants, I’ve taught you the unconditioned and the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.12:1.10.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, satthārā karaṇīyaṁ sāvakānaṁ hitesinā anukampakena anukampaṁ upādāya, kataṁ vo taṁ mayā.
sn43.12:1.10.eng: Out of sympathy, I’ve done what a teacher should do who wants what’s best for their disciples.

sn43.12:1.11.pli: Etāni, bhikkhave, rukkhamūlāni, etāni suññāgārāni. Jhāyatha, bhikkhave, mā pamādattha; mā pacchā vippaṭisārino ahuvattha. Ayaṁ vo amhākaṁ anusāsanīti.
sn43.12:1.11.eng: Here are these roots of trees, and here are these empty huts. Practice absorption, mendicants! Don’t be negligent! Don’t regret it later! This is my instruction to you.”

sn43.12:2.1.pli: Asaṅkhatañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi asaṅkhatagāmiñca maggaṁ.
sn43.12:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, I will teach you the unconditioned and the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.12:2.4.eng: The ending of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn43.12:2.6.eng: And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.12:2.8.eng: This is called the path that leads to the unconditioned. …”

sn43.12:2.9.pli: Iti kho, bhikkhave, desitaṁ vo mayā asaṅkhataṁ …pe…

sn43.12:3.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.12:3.2.eng: Immersion with placing the mind and keeping it connected. …

sn43.12:3.5.eng: Immersion without placing the mind, merely keeping it connected. …

sn43.12:3.8.pli: Avitakko avicāro samādhi. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.12:3.8.eng: Immersion without placing the mind or keeping it connected. …

sn43.12:4.8.pli: Appaṇihito samādhi. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….

sn43.12:5.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
sn43.12:5.2.eng: A mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world. …

sn43.12:5.5.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vedanāsu vedanānupassī viharati …pe…
sn43.12:5.5.eng: A mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of feelings …

sn43.12:5.8.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu citte cittānupassī viharati …pe…
sn43.12:5.8.eng: A mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the mind …

sn43.12:5.11.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati …pe… ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.12:5.11.eng: A mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of principles …

sn43.12:6.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anuppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
sn43.12:6.2.eng: A mendicant generates enthusiasm, tries, makes an effort, exerts the mind, and strives so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise. …

sn43.12:6.5.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
sn43.12:6.5.eng: A mendicant generates enthusiasm, tries, makes an effort, exerts the mind, and strives so that bad, unskillful qualities are given up. …

sn43.12:6.8.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anuppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ uppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
sn43.12:6.8.eng: A mendicant generates enthusiasm, tries, makes an effort, exerts the mind, and strives so that skillful qualities arise. …

sn43.12:6.11.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.12:6.11.eng: A mendicant generates enthusiasm, tries, makes an effort, exerts the mind, and strives so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are fulfilled by development. …

sn43.12:7.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu chandasamādhipadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti.
sn43.12:7.2.eng: A mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort. …

sn43.12:7.5.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vīriyasamādhipadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti.
sn43.12:7.5.eng: A mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy …

sn43.12:7.8.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cittasamādhipadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti.

sn43.12:7.11.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vīmaṁsasamādhipadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.12:7.11.eng: immersion due to inquiry, and active effort. …

sn43.12:8.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddhindriyaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn43.12:8.2.eng: A mendicant develops the faculty of faith, which relies on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripens as letting go. …

sn43.12:8.5.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vīriyindriyaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ …pe…
sn43.12:8.5.eng: A mendicant develops the faculty of energy …

sn43.12:8.14.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paññindriyaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.12:8.14.eng: wisdom, which relies on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripens as letting go. …

sn43.12:9.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddhābalaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ …pe…
sn43.12:9.2.eng: A mendicant develops the power of faith …

sn43.12:9.14.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paññābalaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.12:9.14.eng: wisdom, which relies on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripens as letting go. …

sn43.12:10.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ …pe…
sn43.12:10.2.eng: A mendicant develops the awakening factor of mindfulness …

sn43.12:10.10.pli: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.12:10.10.eng: equanimity, which relies on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripens as letting go. …

sn43.12:11.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.

sn43.12:11.11.pli: asaṅkhatañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi asaṅkhatagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.12:11.15.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn43.12:11.15.eng: right immersion, which relies on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripens as letting go.

sn43.12:11.16.eng: This is called the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.12:11.17.pli: Iti kho, bhikkhave, desitaṁ vo mayā asaṅkhataṁ, desito asaṅkhatagāmimaggo.
sn43.12:11.17.eng: So, mendicants, I’ve taught you the unconditioned and the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.12:11.18.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, satthārā karaṇīyaṁ sāvakānaṁ hitesinā anukampakena anukampaṁ upādāya, kataṁ vo taṁ mayā.
sn43.12:11.18.eng: Out of sympathy, I’ve done what a teacher should do who wants what’s best for their disciples.

sn43.12:11.19.pli: Etāni, bhikkhave, rukkhamūlāni, etāni suññāgārāni. Jhāyatha, bhikkhave, mā pamādattha; mā pacchā vippaṭisārino ahuvattha. Ayaṁ vo amhākaṁ anusāsanī”ti.
sn43.12:11.19.eng: Here are these roots of trees, and here are these empty huts. Practice absorption, mendicants! Don’t be negligent! Don’t regret it later! This is my instruction to you.”

sn43.13:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, I will teach you the uninclined …”

sn43.13:1.4.eng: (Tell in full as in the unconditioned in the previous chapter.)

sn43.14:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, I will teach you the undefiled …

sn43.15:1.1.pli: Saccañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi saccagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.16:1.1.pli: Pārañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi pāragāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.17:1.1.pli: Nipuṇañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi nipuṇagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.18:1.1.pli: Sududdasañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi sududdasagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.19:1.1.pli: Ajajjarañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi ajajjaragāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.20:1.1.pli: Dhuvañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi dhuvagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.21:1.1.pli: Apalokitañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi apalokitagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.22:1.1.pli: Anidassanañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi anidassanagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.23:1.1.pli: Nippapañcañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi nippapañcagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.24:1.1.pli: Santañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi santagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.25:1.1.pli: Amatañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi amatagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.26:1.1.pli: Paṇītañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi paṇītagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.27:1.1.pli: Sivañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi sivagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.28:1.1.pli: Khemañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi khemagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.29:1.1.pli: Taṇhākkhayañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi taṇhākkhayagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.30:1.1.pli: Acchariyañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi acchariyagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.31:1.1.pli: Abbhutañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi abbhutagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.32:1.1.pli: Anītikañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi anītikagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.33:1.1.pli: Anītikadhammañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi anītikadhammagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.34:1.1.pli: Nibbānañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi nibbānagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.35:1.1.pli: Abyābajjhañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi abyābajjhagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.36:1.1.pli: Virāgañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi virāgagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.37:1.1.pli: Suddhiñca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi suddhigāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.37:1.2.pli: Taṁ suṇātha. Katamā ca, bhikkhave, suddhi …pe….

sn43.38:1.1.pli: Muttiñca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi muttigāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.38:1.2.pli: Taṁ suṇātha. Katamā ca, bhikkhave, mutti …pe….

sn43.39:1.1.pli: Anālayañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi anālayagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.39:1.2.pli: Taṁ suṇātha. Katamo ca, bhikkhave, anālayo …pe….

sn43.40:1.1.pli: Dīpañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi dīpagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.41:1.1.pli: Leṇañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi leṇagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.42:1.1.pli: Tāṇañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi tāṇagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.43:1.1.pli: Saraṇañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi saraṇagāmiñca maggaṁ.

sn43.2:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, I will teach you the unconditioned and the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.2:1.4.eng: The ending of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn43.2:1.6.eng: And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.2:1.8.eng: This is called the path that leads to the unconditioned. …”

sn43.3:0.3.eng: Placing the Mind and Keeping it Connected

sn43.3:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.3:1.2.pli: Savitakkasavicāro samādhi, avitakkavicāramatto samādhi, avitakkaavicāro samādhi—ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.3:1.2.eng: Immersion with placing the mind and keeping it connected. Immersion without placing the mind, merely keeping it connected. Immersion without placing the mind or keeping it connected. …”

sn43.4:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.4:1.2.pli: Suññato samādhi, animitto samādhi, appaṇihito samādhi—ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.4:1.2.eng: Emptiness immersion; signless immersion; undirected immersion. …”

sn43.44:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, I will teach you the haven and the path that leads to the haven.

sn43.44:1.4.eng: The ending of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn43.44:1.6.eng: And what is the path that leads to the haven?

sn43.44:1.8.eng: This is called the path that leads to the haven.

sn43.44:1.9.pli: Iti kho, bhikkhave, desitaṁ vo mayā parāyanaṁ, desito parāyanagāmimaggo.
sn43.44:1.9.eng: So, mendicants, I’ve taught you the haven and the path that leads to the haven.

sn43.44:1.10.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, satthārā karaṇīyaṁ sāvakānaṁ hitesinā anukampakena anukampaṁ upādāya, kataṁ vo taṁ mayā.
sn43.44:1.10.eng: Out of sympathy, I’ve done what a teacher should do who wants what’s best for their disciples.

sn43.44:1.11.pli: Etāni, bhikkhave, rukkhamūlāni, etāni suññāgārāni. Jhāyatha, bhikkhave, mā pamādattha; mā pacchā vippaṭisārino ahuvattha. Ayaṁ vo amhākaṁ anusāsanī”ti.
sn43.44:1.11.eng: Here are these roots of trees, and here are these empty huts. Practice absorption, mendicants! Don’t be negligent! Don’t regret it later! This is my instruction to you.”

sn43.44:1.12.eng: (Tell in full as in the unconditioned.)

sn43.44:4.5.eng: The Linked Discourses on the unconditioned are complete.

sn43.5:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.5:1.2.pli: Cattāro satipaṭṭhānā. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.5:1.2.eng: The four kinds of mindfulness meditation. …”

sn43.6:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.6:1.2.pli: Cattāro sammappadhānā. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….

sn43.7:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.7:1.2.pli: Cattāro iddhipādā. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
sn43.7:1.2.eng: The four bases of psychic power. …”

sn43.8:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.9:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.9:1.2.pli: Pañca balāni. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….
